subject: Human Resources
title: Retirement Plans and 401(k)
description: Learn Retirement Plans and 401(k) in one minute.

hook.text: A company matches 401(k) contributions up to 6%. An employee who doesn't contribute is leaving free money on the table.

buildup.text: Retirement plans—like 401(k)s and pensions—help employees save for the future, often with employer contributions.

discovery.text: The 401(k) was born from a 1978 tax code provision. It shifted retirement risk from employers to employees.

twist.text: Traditional pensions guaranteed income for life. 401(k)s depend on individual decisions—and many people save too little.

climax.text: Auto-enrollment and employer matching dramatically increase participation—behavioral nudges fix what education alone cannot.

punchline.text: The best retirement plan is the one people actually use.

quiz.question: How did the 401(k) change retirement?
options: It shifted retirement savings risk from employers to employees
It eliminated the need for saving
